artPrompt: An evocative scene of a hulking green sludge-mass looming over a crooked reservoir-side ballot box, extending a dripping pseudopod to stuff a blank illegible ballot inside, bioluminescent ooze trailing across rusted pipes beneath a bruised stormy sky. It votes for whatever feeds it most. The brooding ooze fills the wide frame, the lone ballot box small at its base. Wide 4:3 landscape, Gruul murky green and rust red, toxic menace, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: The Thing Votes for Itself
text:
Whenever any player votes, Big Murph or a creature you control of your choice also casts a vote, and its vote counts as a number of votes equal to its sludge counters. It usually wins. It votes for whatever option feeds it most.
If the thing's votes alone decide any vote at the table, put three sludge counters on it and announce the results as both the candidate and the returning officer, simultaneously.
flavorText: Write-in candidate, every election, every office, every year. Ballot smelled of algae.
